mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 305, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real gross domestic product growth reflects healthy aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, consistently generating positive monthly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns over rolling 5-year windows, or mean returns are negative when GDP growth exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates manufacturing sector fatigue and reduced capacity utilization, prompting investors to devalue cyclical equities and generating negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO falls below 100 during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
